drink.name: Homemade lemonade
drink.reason: Specifically recommended by a 5-star reviewer; only named drink with direct praise.
drink.quote: Would definitely recommend trying their homemade lemonade

food.name: Breakfast / general menu
food.reason: Food is repeatedly praised as 'amazing' and 'tasty' across multiple high-star reviews, but no specific dish is named.
food.quote: Amazing food, great atmosphere

reviewSample.text: Absolutely loved Café Puaa, i came everyday during my time visiting Tel Aviv! The coffee is lovely, coming from an italian! The service is good, place gets packed so you might have to wait a bit, but everyone is lovely! Would definitely recommend trying their homemade lemonade 🫶🏽

I’m so so sorry to say this, I love the place, I love the vibe, location and food is great, but when I went back the way the hostesses treated us was absolutely atrocious. Horrible behaviour. Rude stares. Were upset and appalled that we dared to interrupt their conversation with one another.  Barely wanted to do their jobs to seat us. Honestly, it was high school bully behaviour. We were given the bare minimum, seated, menus thrown at the table as if doing us a favour, rolled their eyes at us, we left immediately. Which is a shame because I do love the establishment. This isn’t how hostesses, the welcomers and the face of the restaurant should behave.

I have visited this place several times a few years ago and was happy to return again.
It has become a much larger place, taking over a few of the places next door.
The design is still very hipster, 70s vibe, eclectic and lovely. Very popular among the tourists around the glee market.
The food is also still very tasty, but they did raise the rates quite a bit and for some of the dishes I do think the value for money is not great.

drink.name: Beer
drink.reason: Mentioned positively in two reviews, with one specifically calling it 'great and cheap.' Espresso and coffee mentions were brief and generic.
drink.quote: served great and cheap beer

food.name: Breakfast / Egg dishes
food.reason: The scrambled egg/breakfast dishes are praised across multiple reviews, called 'amazing breakfast' and 'tasty,' with specific mention of the egg dish with bacon.
food.quote: amazing breakfast (they have scrambled egg dishes)

reviewSample.text: Nice bar! We went here three times during our stay in TLV. I liked the bruschetta and espresso. My friends tried the burger and enjoyed it. The homey breakfast was tasty, but the portion of eggs was not that big. I expected a bit more to be honest. The staff was very friendly though! When they forgot the apple juice, they gave it to us on the house:) The atmosphere is really cozy. A true hidden gem in the Florentine neighbourhood.

Hidden gem, good coffee, amazing breakfast (they have scrambled egg dishes), vegan and meat friendly, outdoor seating divided into a table type service + couches and indoor.

Their egg dish w/ bacon was nice, I asked for a spicy sauce which they provided and made this bread type sandwich.

They have a bar which looks to tailor to the afternoon and evening crew, menus in English and Hebrew.

A few people posting up with laptops so looks to be laptop friendly.

I’ll be coming back a few more times.

Just need to find out if they have kombucha.
Great food with excellent atmosphere. I enjoy my brunch with a relaxing vibe here. Staffs are friendly and serve customer in a good manner. Would definitely come back if visit Florentine again :)

drink.name: Cold brew
drink.reason: Explicitly rated 5/5 in a 5-star review; other reviews praise coffee generally but only cold brew is named with specific high praise.
drink.quote: cold brew was 5/5

food.name: Pistachio cake
food.reason: The only specifically named food item, described as delicious in a 5-star review.
food.quote: Pistachio cake was delicious

reviewSample.text: One of my favorite coffee spots in TLV. Very chill corner with outdoor seating. Great coffee. If the benches are full, grab a cup and walk across the street to the tiny park with lots of benches. Also has possibly the best sweatshirt logo merch of any coffee shop.

Nice and friendly place. The coffee was good and the food was alright. My only real complaint is the tiny coffee cups. Please have normal big TA cups.  It might sound like a small issue but that's enough of a reason to choose a different place for my morning routine.

I loved this place. Pistachio cake was delicious and cold brew was 5/5. Great place to sit and chill while you’re enjoying your morning coffee. Looked like there were a lot of remote workers there posted up as well. Highly recommend!

drink.name: Coffee
drink.reason: Mentioned positively as reasonably priced, but no specific coffee drink is named.
drink.quote: the coffee is reasonable

food.name: Malabi
food.reason: Overwhelmingly the signature item—praised across nearly every review as delicious, with customizable syrups and toppings.
food.quote: The most delicious malabi in the city

reviewSample.text: If you are craving an oriental dessert then Hamalabia is the trendy place for you.  At the corner in the center of the flea market in Old Jaffa.  You can have  a cold  beer, coffee, chasers and other kinds of desserts.  with the Tel Aviv youth

If you like hipster places with filthy tables, servers that ignore you and then in the middle of taking your order just do something else. Then this is the place to be. They play loud music that Shazam cannot recognize. The only person enjoying the music was the person who chose it. The plus side is the coffee is reasonable and they are well known for their malabi and hipster vibe. If cleanliness and not dying from septicemia is your thing bring your own table wipes.
As usual, good malabi and friendly staff. There’s an open space to hang around or a small area with air conditioning on the side. They have wifi so it’s the perfect spot to chill and work from.

drink.name: Coffee
drink.reason: Coffee is mentioned as available but no specific drink is praised or detailed by reviewers.
drink.quote: They got coffee and other soft drinks

food.name: Chausson aux pommes
food.reason: Specifically praised in detail with high enthusiasm; flaky buttery dough and perfectly balanced apple filling highlighted in a 5-star review. Pastries overall are the standout at this bakery.
food.quote: pastry dough was so flaky & buttery... the absolute perfect bite

reviewSample.text:
An amazing bakery & cafe!
They make & bake all the food right on the spot, and you can actually watch the bakers, which is a mesmerizing sight at itself.

They specialize in all sort of special pastries, which made it very hard to choose from, but eventually I went for one the chocolate chip cookies (the one with chocolate milk, caramel & pecans) & the chausson au pommes (which is one of my favorite all time pastries, when done right).

The pastry dough of the chausson au pommes was so flaky & buttery, and the apple puree inside was delicious, while not too sweet, which created together the absolute perfect bite.

The cookie was indulgent, sweet from the chocolate and salty from the caramel, with the nutty accents from the pecan - a total heaven.

I will sure come back to try more of the amazing variety they offer!!

We came an hour before closing time which meant that there wasn’t as much of a selection, but we got 50% off one of the items which was a definitely a benefit to coming late. We tried the chocolate cake and the pastry that was made up of croissant pastry, peacans, dates and chocolate. Both were really yummy. The chocolate basque cake was very rich and had sort of a caramel in parts of it which was nice, and the croissant pastry mix was really unique, not like anything I’ve tried before. Because we came before closing, we didn’t have the option to sit there but the hamasila park is only a few minutes walk away and a perfect location for a little picnic. Would love to come back earlier in the day and see what else is for sale!

Solid 10 out of 10. Hand made, fresh, perfectly done, inovative pastries. Great service and clean well designed location. Only bad thing is the high prices.
I will be back for sure.

drink.name: Wine
drink.reason: Mentioned positively alongside the stellar meal, but no specific wine named.
drink.quote: such good wine too

food.name: Sea Bass Fillet
food.reason: Praised in multiple 5-star reviews as a standout main dish.
food.quote: the sea bass entree was a close second

reviewSample.text: Such a wonderful establishment and restaurant with a great atmosphere and energy. Yuval was extremely friendly and polite towards me and the server Tal was simply one of the best. I would highly recommend Cafe Cucu to anyone visiting in Israel or Tel Aviv. The food is very fresh and delicious too.

I really wanted to try this place, hesitated between brunch and dinner menu, chose the later.
Unfortunately, I was so disappointed. I expected dishes to be precise and neat, but they looked like they were made by an amateur, meat in tartar and meat cut was of very poor quality, also tartar looked very pale and cream of egg yolk just didn't work on it.
One of the worst cheesecakes I have every tried.
The positive side is Happy hour from 17:00 till 19:00 with 30% off on everything.

When I say amazing food, I mean it! A menu of simplistic but absolutely delicious food. Everything was fresh to order and service was great. We ordered their Beef Tartare & Pickled Yolk Cream, Tomatoes & Mozzarella Salad, and their main Sea Bass Fillet! One of the top restaurants I added to my Tel Aviv restaurant list!

drink.name: Espresso
drink.reason: Multiple reviews praise the espresso and coffee quality; one 5-star review specifically calls out excellent espresso, and another highlights a great cortado.
drink.quote: Origem has excellent espresso and pour over

food.name: Sandwich
food.reason: Sandwiches are mentioned by two reviewers, with one specifically praising a cheese, tomato, and lettuce sandwich on rye bread.
food.quote: sandwiches with cheese, tomato, and lettuce on rye bread

reviewSample.text: Origem has excellent espresso and pour over. Always a couple tasty bakery and dessert items. They’ve moved next door to their old location which is much larger and offers great seating both inside and out under the trees.

Charming small cafe in the old north part of the city. the staff and the owner are from Brazil and they super nice people, you can find here many blends of great coffee. the atmosphere at the cafe is fantastic, so relaxing and the music perfect + great taste of decoration. they have also some sandwiches and sweets.
I really like this place !

Highly recommended !

This place is really cute and small. It’s perfect for someone who either wants to take a coffee and go or someone who would like to sit down with a book or do work on a laptop. Is very relaxing and can be quiet.

There is also outdoor seating and they have heaters on a chillier day that you can ask to use if needed.

It’s a lovely clean coffee shop with a few food options that are nice like sandwiches with cheese, tomato, and lettuce on rye bread. I don’t even like rye bread but it did compliment a sandwich I shared with a friend so I’m impressed!

The pastries they had all looked great, they had cookies and different types of cakes.

The staff was very kind but informed me that they couldn’t make a cold brew because the machine was not working. I wasn’t able to get the coffee I had hoped for because I don’t like hot coffee but I’m hopeful for the next time I come that it will be repaired.

This place is a blend of Brasília and Israel apparently which I thought was cool! I love the idea of fusion in the same place.

I would come back here but just remember you might not be able to get your cold coffee if that’s what you were looking for. I’m sure it will be fixed by the time you read this though!

drink.name: House coffee
drink.reason: Coffee is mentioned but with mixed/generic feedback—one reviewer says it's good, another calls it average. No specific drink is named.
drink.quote: The food is good, and so is the coffee

food.name: Brisket sandwich
food.reason: Specifically praised in detail in a 5-star review as 'truly excellent' with high-quality brisket bursting with flavor. Other food mentions are more general.
food.quote: the brisket sandwich and it was truly excellent

reviewSample.text: Delicious brunch in the Carmel market area. Typical Israeli dishes full of flavour and very generous. The place has outdoor as well as indoor seating space and the vibe is really cool and welcoming. Staff were super friendly too.

Some of the best food I've eaten in Israel! I ordered the brisket sandwich and it was truly excellent. The brisket was of a high quality, bursting with flavor and heat (not spicy, but temperature-wise). The pickles added a great touch as well. The other members of my party had the smoked salmon and avocado sandwich which they said was excellent. Two people tried the chocolate chip cookie (cookie) which was okay. It was better once they asked it to be warmed up.
Wow, so good! Came in on a rainy day and were happy to find out they have a cozy indoor space. The food was exceptionally fresh and service impeccable. Will come back again, possibly tomorrow:-)

drink.name: Pumpkin Spice Latte
drink.reason: Explicitly called out as a seasonal must-try in a 5-star review. Coffee reviews are mixed overall (one reviewer compared regular coffee to instant), so the specifically praised seasonal drink is the safer pick.
drink.quote: Seasonal must-try: the pumpkin spice latte

food.name: Israeli Breakfast
food.reason: Praised as delicious and homemade in multiple 5-star reviews, with reviewers specifically highlighting the authentic Israeli breakfast experience. Homemade sandwiches also got strong mentions.
food.quote: authentic Israeli Breakfast... delicious and homemade

reviewSample.text: We enjoyed so much the authentic Israeli Breakfast at this cozy neighborhood cafe! Ot was pouring rain, the servers and the host were super friendly and made room for us to accommodate. The food is delicious and homemade. Highly recommended place!

Perfect neighborhood spot for breakfast 🥐☕️

I’ve seen moms meeting here with their newborns, they welcome dogs with so much love, prices are fair and the staff is just cool vibes only. Definitely a local café that belongs in your top 5 – sandwiches are homemade, super fresh and seriously delicious.

We came for a cowork session with the girls, they gave us the biggest table and treated us like queens 👸🏼👸🏼👸🏼
Seasonal must-try: the pumpkin spice latte – only for this season 🥳

Thank you Café Shneor! 🤍

This place is absolutely charming — gorgeous setting, cozy atmosphere, and top-notch service. The food was a delight, beautifully made and plated with care. But then came the coffee… and it broke the spell. It tasted like instant coffee straight out of a teacher’s lounge — a real buzzkill after such a great experience.

drink.name: Frozen chai/coffee
drink.reason: Singled out as a main reason to visit; cappuccino also praised in two 5-star reviews, but the frozen chai/coffee received the most enthusiastic specific praise.
drink.quote: frozen chai / coffee is amazing

food.name: Lentil and sweet potato salad
food.reason: Praised in two separate 5-star reviews as terrific and tasty, making it the most consistently recommended food item.
food.quote: Great lentil sweet potato salad

reviewSample.text: I had a really unpleasant experience at this cafe. I was almost done with my coffee, quietly working on my notebook, when a staff member rudely told me I had to leave because “no notebooks are allowed today”. The absurd part is that other customers were sitting right there with their notebooks and nobody said a word to them 🤷‍♀️ So the rule only applied to me, I guess. Definitely won’t be coming back.
Great lentil sweet potato salad.   Good vibe. Good bread products. Efficient friendly service. Definitely above most. And good cappuccino too.

I love this place, but not for the coffee.. the flat white was crazy acidic and burnt. I’ve had their iced latte which is average. Their frozen chai / coffee is amazing and one of the main reasons I stop by. The other reason I stop by, is that it’s a great place to get some work done. Lots of seating indoors and outdoors. They also have some good sandwiches and food options but I haven’t tried them.

TLDR come for the frozen chai/coffee or lunch or getting some remote work done. Free wifi.

If you want a proper flat white, go down the street to p.o.c.

drink.name: Coffee
drink.reason: Coffee is praised across nearly every review as 'very good,' 'delicious,' and 'amazing,' though no specific drink type is named.
drink.quote: The coffee is amazing as well

food.name: Salmon and cream cheese bagel
food.reason: The salmon/lox bagel is specifically named and praised in multiple high-star reviews, making it the most consistently recommended food item.
food.quote: Great salmon bagels!

reviewSample.text:
If like me, you're looking to try as much of the mind-blowing and taste redefining food that fills most of Tel-Aviv's street, and you find yourself, on a Saturday morning, just having to try the Jachnun (whether because you crave it or its your last day in town and you just haven't tried it yet), then by all means, this place is your spot.

Lovely and cozy place. Chill vibe, and most importantly, open on the shabbat! (Saturday mornings)

They of course have coffee, bagels, juices and whatever else obe might fancy if the Jachnun is not to your taste!

Cheerio!
Coffee and bagels. Very warm reception. Awesome place to sit among the locals, just off the Carmel Shuk main street so it's rather quiet to sit there while you can observe the bustling market goers.

Great salmon bagels! The coffee was very good, too. We came back several times during our trip, and everything was great every time. The owner/waiter was really nice! Highly recommend for breakfast and/or coffee while you‘re at the market.

drink.name: Iced Latte
drink.reason: Specifically praised in a 5-star review for its fresh, fruity, refreshing flavor; multiple reviews highlight excellent specialty coffee and well-roasted beans that pair well with milk hot or cold.
drink.quote: Iced latte with a fresh, fruity flavor

food.name: Scandinavian/Danish Bun (saffron-almond or pistachio)
food.reason: The bun is the most consistently and specifically praised food item, mentioned across three reviews with explicit recommendations for the saffron/almond and pistachio cream versions.
food.quote: get the saffron / almond bun, worth it!

reviewSample.text:
Best coffee I’ve tasted so far in Tel Aviv.

The existing crowd outside the coffeeshop did not deter me from walking straight into the shop toward the end of the line. And the cafe turns out to be quite spacious inside.

Iced latte with a fresh, fruity flavor. Although I don’t usually like milk coffee on the sour side, this one is really refreshing.

My Scandinavian bun is made from a springy dough, topped with crispy almond flakes and filled with a perfect amount of pistachio cream.
Delicious coffee and chill atmosphere, totally recommend getting the saffron / almond bun, worth it! Also noticed that regulars have their own cups up on a shelf, that’s such a great idea.

I had a great time here today. I had a great espresso. It was fruity, light, soft, and was barely bitter which I love. I chose to sit both on the inner bar, that looks to the very small courtyard outside, and on the exterior bar, that looks onto the street. Both were beautiful and calm. The staff were generous and sweet, and the cookie I had was alright haha.
In general, apart from the fact that a cold espresso cost me 14 NIS, the experience was perfect. Practical, calm, tasty.

Thank you!

drink.name: Grey Blend Coffee
drink.reason: Specifically praised in a 5-star review for excellent crema, oil, and temperature; general coffee quality is the standout across nearly every review.
drink.quote: Best damn coffee in Israel... crema is good, oil is good, temp is good

food.reason: No food item received positive reviews. The chocolate chip cookie was called dry and not tasty, and sandwiches were said to need upgrading.

reviewSample.text: I had a great experience at Wake Up Coffee. The coffee was excellent, fresh, and full of flavor, and the atmosphere was relaxed and comfortable, perfect for starting the day or taking a short break. The staff was friendly and efficient, and everything from the service to the quality of the drinks made it a place I would definitely come back to.

Updating 😁 it’s been a while, I hope that now everybody is welcome as I do like this place.

Unfortunately I had to change my review, i’ve been thrown away after sitting in this coffee shop many times saying that “ you can’t get in if you eating non-kosher food” 😱
I find it very disturbing when people allow themselves to be racist or homophobic or whatever extreme idea it was.
The same way that I would never tell somebody that he cannot get into the place because he only eats kosher food it is not OK to say the opposite if my views are different.
I decided to put three stars instead of one to allow them to apologize and civilized.

One of my favorite coffee shops in Tel Aviv, i’d like it because it’s both casual and very chic.
I love spending time there at my morning and have my luxury coffee :) it’s super tasty and super high-quality.
The coffee is deep in flavor and made to perfection.
They should definitely upgrade their sandwiches but nothing will stop me from grabbing another cup of coffee at this place.
I just love it!

The coffee is very good, and the place is really nice. However, the chocolate chip cookie I tried was quite dry and not so tasty. The price is average to a specialty coffee in Tel Aviv. I had bought some beans which were great, but when I had asked whether it's fare trade the cashier had no idea what I was talking about. So I'm pretty sure it's not fare trade;) Nice place

drink.name: Iced chocolate
drink.reason: Specifically praised in a 5-star review for using a milk and dark chocolate mix rather than overly sweet powder, standing out from other Tel Aviv cafes.
drink.quote: not too sweet at all... the thought behind the drink is there

food.name: Rugelach
food.reason: Called the best ever in an enthusiastic 5-star review. Other food items (focaccia, almond croissant) were praised but described as more standard.
food.quote: Best rugelach I've ever had

reviewSample.text: I love Neve Tzedek and I’m there often, but I’ve been intentionally avoiding this spot. A staff member was unexpectedly rude to a few of my friends, and I experienced the same thing once as well. The owners did try to apologize, but for me it came too late. I genuinely hope the place changes one day and becomes the kind of warm, good-vibes spot people want to be around.

Really high quality patisserie in Neve Tzedek. We had the iced chocolate, focaccia sandwich and almond croissant. I was pleasantly surprised by the iced chocolate because most of the cafes in Tel Aviv use milk chocolate or powder, and you end up with something quite sweet, rather than chocolatey, whereas here it was not too sweet at all. We spoke with the really friendly barista afterwards and she showed us that the chocolate drinks are made with a mixture of milk and dark chocolate, which is what I’ve been wanting to find in the city. It was slightly on the more bitter side but the thought behind the drink is there for sure. The focaccia was really fresh and simply well-made bread. The almond croissant was a good standard almond croissant, nothing too special about it. They have lots of outdoor seating, both right outside the bakery and across the road where there are picnic benches with plenty of shade. The staff were all very friendly and we had a positive experience there.
The food is good but its packed and looks like they are not suited to deal with so many customers, waited for my coffee 15 min ;(

drink.name: Matcha
drink.reason: Matcha is specifically named multiple times — praised for quality and noted as a regular order, and oat/almond-rice milk options support it.
drink.quote: getting a matcha and a baked good multiple times a week

food.name: Salmon bagel/sandwich
food.reason: The salmon sandwich is the most specifically praised food item in a 5-star review, called a favorite. One negative review dislikes the beer-cured style, so expectations matter.
food.quote: the salmon sandwich with added avocado is my fav

reviewSample.text:
This is my happy place on Ben Yehuda. Every visit feels like a warm hug: The team here genuinely makes you feel at home the moment you arrive. I always end up staying longer than planned.
Food-wise the salmon sandwich with added avocado is my fav, and the egg & cheese is pure comfort done right. Simple things, done really well.
If you’re in Tel Aviv and need a spot that’s got great coffee, honest food, and actual soul — this is it.

Read the most recent reviews and you’ll see a pattern - a handful of good reviews in the middle of a bunch of reviews about rude waiters, poor service, and managers who seem to make things harder for everyone. We are one of those experiences.

Update: Service is sincerely lacking. I ordered their salmon bagel, which was listed as a classic salmon bagel with cream cheese, capers, dill, and salmon. I assumed that meant Smoked Salmon as it is everywhere else on earth, and instead they serve “beer-cured salmon” which has the texture of uncooked raw salmon. Not my taste (and I’m honestly surprised this would be enjoyed by anyone, but to each their own.) I asked to have it replaced with something else since it was not what I expected and didn’t taste good and was told they would only give me a 50% discount and I could pay full price for another dish if I wanted.

My family owned a restaurant for 25 years and this is such a short-sighted decision. I was previously getting a matcha and a baked good multiple times a week and told other people about the quality of the coffee here, and instead of making it right, they have lost a customer for good, all for the price of one dish? 🤷🏼‍♀️

Oh and 2 drinks, 2 dishes and kids pancakes cost 187 shekels and then we were told we couldn’t pay in cash unless we waited for “5-7 minutes” because they could not make us change. Overpriced and overrated. What a shame.

Overpriced and condescending – don't bother.
Extremely disappointing experience. For a place that charges premium prices, the service is incredibly poor and pretentious.
• Service: The staff is slow and has a very dismissive attitude. It felt like I was a burden just for being there.
• The "Sugar" Incident: When I asked them to add sweetener to my coffee, they flat-out refused and told me to do it myself. In any decent cafe in Tel Aviv—especially at these price points—this is a basic service you’d expect.
• Value for Money: Between the unfriendly vibe and the high prices, there is absolutely no reason to visit.
There are plenty of other spots in the city that actually value their customers. I won’t be coming back.

drink.name: Coffee
drink.reason: Multiple reviewers praise the coffee as good, though one complained it was weak and lukewarm. No specific drink type dominates.
drink.quote: the coffee is good and the service is friendly

food.name: Almond croissant
food.reason: Specifically praised for taste and perfect almond cream to croissant ratio. Other food items (Danish, sabich) received negative reviews.
food.quote: almond croissant was really tasty... perfect amount of almond cream to croissant ratio

reviewSample.text: I really love the vibe and atmosphere at this cafe. There’s lots of outdoor seating in the shade and among lots of plants and greenery which is really nice. It also really suits for people who want to work on their laptop, both inside and outside, as you have sort of low bar style seating facing the wall for individuals. I had the almond croissant and an iced chocolate. The almond croissant was really tasty, and not too heavy - the perfect amount of almond cream to croissant ratio. The iced chocolate took a really long time to come and my partner had to ask three times before they made it. They also told him to go and sit and that they would bring it to us, but they just kept forgetting. It’s made with powder rather than chocolate but it was a good quality cocoa powder and it was okay.

A local spot with Florentine vibes in both look and atmosphere. I haven’t tried the food, but the coffee is good and the service is friendly, always with a welcoming smile.

After posting the review, the business responded "What would improve our rating?"
I found the place could benefit from improved cleanliness.

Fantastic new cafe in the heart of Florentin! Their coffee was good and there is plenty of sitting space inside and outside. Also, differently from other Tel Aviv cafes, it’s quite spacious. They also have some food and cake options. Service is friendly.

drink.name: Cold brew
drink.reason: Explicitly praised as locally made and tasting amazing in a 5-star review; other reviews highlight excellent specialty coffee overall.
drink.quote: the cold brew is made locally and tastes amazing

food.name: Banana walnut muffin
food.reason: Directly named as a favorite in a 5-star review, and muffins are separately called 'a big hit' in another 5-star review, while pastries were described as average.
food.quote: The banana walnut muffin was my go to!

reviewSample.text:
One of the best coffee shops in Tel Aviv!!!
Great service, sometimes it could take some time to get your coffee, but all in all, quite ok.
They have a wide selection of coffee (numerous grinders are lined up like soldiers) and the cold brew is made locally and tastes amazing!
The muffins were also a big hit, while the pastries were quite average.
It’s a solid 4.5, but I gave it 5 because of the overall experience (and the assorted mugs).
Unique specialty coffee spot in Tel Aviv, they have a variety of roasted beans, the place looks like a high tech pharmacy, highly recommended for people who appreciate good coffee.

“ - May I have a filtered coffee please”

“ - On friday we don’t have filtered coffee”.

I took my seat, had my coffee, and thought to myself: “maybe they’re short in staff”.

10 min later

- “May I have your wifi please”

“ sorry, we don’t have wifi in fridays”….

…….

drink.reason: No specific drinks are named in the reviews; only general mentions of a coffee shop and alcoholic happy hour.

food.name: Roasted Salad
food.reason: Specifically praised in a 4-star review for its surprising, fresh flavors and ginger dressing. Other named dishes (bruschetta, shakshuka) had complaints.
food.quote: I ate the Roasted Salad and it was very special

reviewSample.text:
What stood out most to me was the friendly and attentive service by the host.  He was genuinely friendly and caring.
Please note that they have a "happy hour" on alcoholic beverages as well as a special lunch menu from noon till 4pm.
We arrived at 3:45 pm but missed it since it took them till after 4pm to take our order.

Once an easy choice for a filling, enjoyable and affordable meal has now lost all its charm.
The service is mediocre, the food portions are small and not filling and pricey for what they deliver. For a small comparison a few years ago at the same cafe you could get double the size of the meal for 50-60nis whereas today you pay 70-80nis for a smaller size of the same dish. Now cafe dizengoff is just a name and has lost its past charm.

What stood out most to me was the friendly and attentive service by the host.  He was genuinely friendly and caring.
Please note that they have a "happy hour" on alcoholic beverages as well as a special lunch menu from noon till 4pm.
We arrived at 3:45 pm but missed it since it took them till after 4pm to take our order.

drink.name: Cappuccino
drink.reason: Praised across multiple reviews for taste and presentation, though one noted small portion size.
drink.quote: cappuccino which were served in beautiful glassware

food.name: Egg sandwich
food.reason: Called 'legendary' and 'simple and delicious' across multiple high-star reviews.
food.quote: the legendary scrambled eggs sandwich - it's simple and delicious

reviewSample.text: Fantastic coffee shop in the heart of Tel Aviv. The design is very clean and minimalist based on a Japanese aesthetic. The coffee was superb. We had espresso and cappuccino which were served in beautiful glassware. We also ate the Japanese omelette on milk bread which was fantastic - very tasty.

Overall a good cafe. I cannot give it a 5 star rating due to the customer service. The girl serving was the least friendly person i've come across. The food and coffee were good however the cappuccino was on the small side for the price.

Liked everything we ordered: egg sandwich, cinnamon and chocolate cream patissier pastries. Great vibe and pretty sitting.
For me cappuccino was very good. I didn't understand the special black coffee, but I think it is because I'm not a pro.

drink.name: Cappuccino
drink.reason: Cappuccino is mentioned by name in multiple 5-star reviews with specific praise for being perfectly balanced and smooth.
drink.quote: perfectly smooth, with just the right balance of espresso, milk, and foam

food.name: Brownie cookie with peanut butter
food.reason: Specifically named and enthusiastically praised in a 5-star review; other reviewers praise pastries generally but this is the only specific standout.
food.quote: brownie cookie with peanut butter in the center? SO good

reviewSample.text:
Wow, this coffee was outstanding! The barista made it look effortless, but the cappuccino he crafted was truly one of a kind. It was perfectly smooth, with just the right balance of espresso, milk, and foam. If you’re a coffee lover looking for a real treat, you absolutely need to order something here.

And the brownie cookie with peanut butter in the center? SO good. Definitely don’t skip it!

LOVE IT!
It’s 2025 upgraded excellent coffee shop.
Smoking free, attitude free, and lots of free love.
Food is wonderful and the coffee is perfect.
When all the coffee shops are closed, this one probably still open, which makes it so convenient.

It’s 2022 and Unfortunately this place still allows smoking 🚬 inside

————————————————-

With great regret,
I must take off at least 2 stars for:

Love this place!

Great new coffee shop at the heart of the most hip neighborhoods in Tel Aviv
Amazing coffee, flavored and made to perfection.
I’m planning to try and go there once a week until I can try all their amazing pastries 😝
Everything looked unique and delicious.
I tried in there chocolate bun end it was absolutely amazing.
YUM

It’s a great spot spots in Tel Aviv! It has a unique, local charm with great outdoor seating that’s perfect for relaxing and people-watching. The staff are always friendly and provide excellent service, even when it gets busy.
What I especially love is the community vibe — there are a lot of regulars, and you’ll often see people enjoying their coffee with their dogs, which adds such a warm and welcoming atmosphere. The coffee and pastries are consistently great too.
Highly recommend p.o.c cafe if you’re looking for a cozy, dog-friendly place with great service and a real neighbourhood feel.

drink.name: Iced Latte
drink.reason: Specifically named as a go-to choice, though one reviewer found Jaffa's slightly weak. Coffee quality overall highly praised with arabica bean choices.
drink.quote: always go for some iced coffee when outside on a hot day

food.name: Pastries
food.reason: Only one specific food mention, and another reviewer noted the food offering is very limited.
food.quote: Great coffee. Great pastries. Highly recommend.

reviewSample.text: I had some great coffee and a good time here. Chill, perfectly situated next to a lovely park, sunny, AC on, they even have cute shelves with the regulars favorite cups and their names on it. Adorable. Thank you!
Very tasty coffe. You can choose from 2 typy beans. 100% arabica. And very nice and pleasant barista
We had a real nice cup of coffee here. The food offering here is very limited. Seating is also very limited. But if coffee is your thing, this is the place.

drink.name: Pour over coffee
drink.reason: Specifically praised in a 5-star review highlighting freshly roasted beans and professional brewing methods including Fellow pour over, flat white, and espresso.
drink.quote: freshly roasted coffee is delicious & the barista service professional

food.reason: Pastries and cakes are mentioned but only generically, with one reviewer calling them 'OK but not great' and no specific food item is named or praised.

reviewSample.text: Slightly expensive but top quality coffee. You pay for quality and atmosphere. This place is a great spot for those who appreciate coffee and desire a refined experience

Adjacent to Dizengoff Circle, Tel Aviv. Nahat speciality coffee shop offers excellent coffee beverages, cakes, pastries and light snacks.
The freshly roasted coffee is delicious & the barista service professional. Choose from the many and varied speciality coffee ☕ freshly roasted beans. All options available in their professional coffee grinders.
From a Fellow pour over, flat white or espresso you will not be disappointed with the brewing method or resulting coffee ☕.

Since they moved from the Disengoff square to a new location, it's been a terrible experience: not cosy, no seats inside, very loud cars, uncomfortable plastic chairs, and it smelled horrible in the back. Coffee is still nice though. But never again

drink.name: Matcha
drink.reason: Mentioned positively in multiple reviews, praised for quality and preparation. Coffee is also highly praised but no specific drink named.
drink.quote: Smooth, vibrant, and not overly sweet

food.name: Shakshuka
food.reason: Mentioned in two reviews with strong praise, including a specific recommendation for the green shakshuka version.
food.quote: So warm, comforting, and clearly made fresh on the spot

reviewSample.text:
Cozy nice little cafe in the corner street with everything you want. My first ever piccolos in Israel (not very common option at other cafes)

Coffee is super nice, I also only ordered a slice of focaccia but the staffs are so nice to help me warm up and add a little flavor to it.

The seats are nice, you get to choose the ones  looks like a school chair with a back, good for my weak back 😭

Came into Cafe Zohar on a rainy, windy winter day just to escape the weather and I’m honestly so glad I did. The atmosphere inside was super chill, the music was perfect, and the people working there were amazing. Service was easily 10/10 from the moment I walked in.

I ordered the shakshuka, and wow… it was exactly what I needed. So warm, comforting, and clearly made fresh on the spot with quality ingredients. Every bite felt homemade and full of flavor.

And the coffee? Hands down one of the best I’ve ever had. Rich, smooth, and made perfectly.

I also tried the matcha, and it was excellent. Smooth, vibrant, and not overly sweet you can really taste the quality. It was prepared beautifully and felt just as thoughtful as everything else on the menu.

If you’re looking for a cozy place to hide from the rain (or just enjoy great food and vibes), Cafe Zohar is definitely the spot. Will 100% be coming back

My friend and I came to this Cafe Zohar in Tel Aviv near Takana Merkazit  and we ordered cold coffee and cold matcha with cinabbon and cookies with triple chocolates and everything was delicious. Everything they serve there is fresh. Servers are so nice and kind. The place itself is just wow. It has great atmosphere where you can just come sit down and work at peace… 💙

drink.name: Iced coffee
drink.reason: Only mentioned in a negative context with service issues; no positive drink endorsements in reviews.
drink.quote: two ice coffes, one with milk and the other with soy milk

food.name: Croque madame toast
food.reason: Highly praised in a 5-star review as huge, loaded with cheese, eggs, and salmon, and includes a drink. Reviews consistently note food is tasty with large shareable portions.
food.quote: 2 big toasts...they were HUGE, with cheese, eggs

reviewSample.text:
The place is located is a great place near the south of Tel Aviv.
There was a line before entering, which took a while, but I guess it is usual on a Friday morning.
The food isn't cheap, bug you are getting large dishes so if is worth the money.
The food itself is tasty, you can probably share some of the dishes and get a desert, that looks good but I was too full to try.
The waiter was nice and responsive and got us everything that we needed in a quick manner.
The place itself was nice, albeit a bit hot, it was a very hot day.

Unfortunately twice bad experiences at this place.
On Independence Day I made a reservation through the app Ontopo. It was accepted and two days before they called from the restaurant to tell that they would be closed in the day. So why they didn't block the day?! I needed to rush to look for another open kosher place.
Obviously Ontopo and the restaurant weren't concerned about it...never came with a kind compensation, they simply thought "not my problem".
Then I said ok, let's try again on Sukkot. I made a reservation at 11:30. When we arrived there was no available table?! So why the reservation?! We waited and seated in a table a meter from the entrance where all people asking to find table talk loudly, so we asked to change table. Why they don't make the restaurant reception much before the corridor to low the noise?!
Food...We are not super demanded on it but, was not hot, the boiled egg still had small pieces of shell that I felt in my mouth. We asked for pepper and it just came after the bill with the table empty, and we asked for two ice coffes, one with milk and the other with soy milk but the waitress didn't know which one was the milk and the soy one...so she returned to the kitchen and just brought back after no more food (at the end didn't charged the coffes, but what ever!).

I walk past this place si many times and have never seen it. It's inside this construction and I always thought was part of the synagogue besides it, but no! It's an amazing place for brunch/lunch! The place is very chill, as it's not so close to the street

The place was pretty full as it was Friday. We ordered 2 big toasts (one was a croque madame, the other I can't remember) and they were HUGE, with cheese, eggs and one had salmon. They both come with a drink included in it I really recommend trying this.

We also ordered a salad with tuna that was very good.

All of this was perfect for 4 people as the dishes are big.

drink.name: Arabic coffee
drink.reason: Mentioned multiple times with specific praise for traditional sand-brewed preparation
drink.quote: coffee, traditionally brewed with hot sand - delicious

food.name: Shakshuka
food.reason: Named specifically in multiple high-star reviews with enthusiastic praise as homemade and amazing
food.quote: shakshuka, a tomato and egg dish that is homemade... it is amazing

reviewSample.text: Great place in Jaffa. Very good food (shakshuka, tabbouleh, kunafa, malabi), good Arabic coffee. Amazing atmosphere of Jaffa and beautiful views. The interior is beautifully decorated. Very much old family trinkets. Nice service. I would highly recommend.

They are the best!!! Food service, music, atmosphere etc. If you want to go back in time, feel yourself again in your grandmother's house full of warmth and tasty food - this is a place where the past becomes a reality. Thank you very much for keeping your family business alive 😘💕🌹

This restaurant is located on the out skirts of Jaffa. Had a wonder meal there and great view of the city, the owner was fantastic and gave me a great history lesson of the area. I would recommend stopping here for lunch or dinner.

drink.name: Coffee
drink.reason: Coffee is praised generically alongside brunch/food, but no specific drink type is named consistently. Banana lassi mentioned once positively.
drink.quote: excellent food and great coffee

food.name: Shakshuka
food.reason: Specifically named and described as delicious in a 5-star review, served with fresh bread and salad at a good price. Other items (vegan shawarma, salads) also praised but shakshuka has the strongest specific endorsement.
food.quote: ordered the shakshuka... everything was delicious

reviewSample.text: Lovely and cosy cafe with very good selection of food, their salads in particular are creative, healthy and tasty. Big outdoor sitting. Perfect for work meetings or socializing or just reading your book. The staff are friendly and attentive.

Cozy bar to chill in the shade outside or inside and have coffee or brunch.
I ordered the shakshuka that comes with fresh bread and a little salad, everything was delicious. The waitress was very friendly. Good price (66 nis for food and coffe).

The Banana lassi was nice. The vegan shawarma was really good, proof that it's all in the seasoning. Other than that all was good (i.e., the location, the atmosphere, the service). The place definitely goes to my list.
Update: The cheese and beetroot salad was very good. The only downside is that people smoke like crazy here, and it ruins the atmosphere.

drink.name: Coffee
drink.reason: Multiple reviewers across high-star reviews praise the coffee as 'damn good' and 'excellent,' though no specific drink is consistently named. Vanilla raf is mentioned once but not tasted.
drink.quote: Damn good coffee!

food.name: Coconut Danish
food.reason: Specifically praised in a 5-star review as a surprising, light, and delicious pastry. Syrniki is mentioned but reviewer admits not tasting it, and the omelette croissant got negative feedback.
food.quote: surprised us as it was light and delicious

reviewSample.text: The place is really nice. Coffee tasted great. And they also have syrniki (didn't taste though, but my friend told those were good). Put 4 for the service simply because there was just one barista this morning so you had to wait a bit. Shouldn't stop you from coming though. Oh, one more thing - they do have vanilla raf.

This is the best place in Tel-Aviv, my absolute beloved one. It’s perfect at all:
1. Damn good coffee!🫡
2. Polite staff, who always can help and of course doing their job professionally 🤲🏻💖
3. Equally very comfortable place for coworking or informal meeting with someone
4. No annoying repetitive music (no music at all and I’m really happy with it, thank you!) that can totally disturb you from whatever you’re doing at the moment 🤩

In conclusion: if you want to have a good coffee and wonderful time, I’m sure you’ll enjoy “Jera”!
For me it will always be the special one. Thank you so much for everything you’ve doing! 🙏🌷

Cute little coffee shop sandwiched between two other coffee shops. The coffee tastes really good, but the omelette croissant is quite tasteless. Two coffee + two little pastries cost more than 80 shekels which is expensive even for Tel Aviv

drink.name: Single Origin Espresso (Ethiopian)
drink.reason: Specifically praised in a 5-star review by a self-described coffee nerd; double shot called excellent with second pull even better. Other reviews mention quality coffee and ceremonial-grade matcha but less specifically.
drink.quote: Second pull was even better than the first

food.reason: Reviewers mention cakes, sweets, and a vegan cookie favorably but no specific food item is named or repeated with enough detail to justify a pick.

reviewSample.text: Had a very good specialty coffee experience here. A single origin espresso was available, Ethiopian natural. The double shot was excellent, but the barista decided to make a second one with a different grind and, liking it better, gave it to me to drink. Medium light roast and delicious. Second pull was even better than the first. Overall really fun for a coffee nerd to chat with baristas who share the interest. Vegan cookie was tasty.

Preferred local cafe whenever I am in TLV  working. Possibly one of the vibiest cafes I know anywhere in the world. It’s a local meeting place where you sit on barrel seats hanging over the edge of the pavement with cars and bikes rolling by. Good coffee and cakes.

This coffee shop is a hidden gem! Along with their yummy sweets and delicious coffee, they also offer free water which is a bonus. The great vibe and outdoor seating area make it a perfect spot to relax and catch up with friends or get some work done. The lovely staff adds to the welcoming atmosphere and makes every visit a pleasure. Overall, I highly recommend this coffee shop to anyone in the area.

drink.name: Espresso (washed Colombian) / Batch Brew
drink.reason: Specifically praised in a 5-star review highlighting Nomena's serious coffee program; multiple reviews emphasize coffee quality and variety of single-origin beans.
drink.quote: delicious batch brew Ethiopian natural... and an excellent espresso

food.name: Chocolate chip cookies
food.reason: Directly named and praised alongside the scrambled egg sandwich in a 5-star review; one other reviewer noted the bakery is sourced externally but didn't try it.
food.quote: chocolate chip cookies are great

reviewSample.text: Great addition to the serious coffee scene in Tel Aviv. I had a delicious batch brew Ethiopian natural (small though), and an excellent espresso (a washed Colombian). Beautiful shop and cafe with lovely outdoor seating and spaces. Great vibe. It was busy!!! Huge variety of beans on sale. Lights roasts and single origins.

This cafe was just OK. I stopped here a handful of times out of convenience on my way to/from my hotel, and I didn't see why it was such a popular place to hang out with your laptop and do work. It was a bit crowded both inside and out on the narrow porch seating.

Decent but pretty small iced matchas, and the guy at the register looked at me like he was always worried about something. Everything here feels small and cramped, from their selection to their seating.

The Tel-Aviv city metro will be running right outside in front of this cafe. I'm curious how that will work out as it seems potentially dangerous.

The best various and flavors coffee in the city!
I know a lot of different coffee houses in tlv but this one is my favorite!
But I didn’t tried the bakery, I didn’t ask about their kashrut, but they order bakery from another place

drink.name: Europe Jasmine cocktail
drink.reason: Specifically praised in a positive review as strong and sweet, with a shoutout to the bartender. Only specifically named drink in the reviews.
drink.quote: strong and sweet. Shoutout to the bartender

food.name: Fish with cornbread
food.reason: Highlighted in a 4-star review with strong praise calling the fish 'so good' and cornbread 'divine.' Seafood focus of menu is reinforced by other reviewers.
food.quote: The fish was so good and the cornbread is divine

reviewSample.text:
It could be easily 5 stars, cause the food was great. The fish was so good and the cornbread is divine.
But - the service was not at his moment.
Some of the waiters were nice, but the others had some attitude.
I will come back and hope for a better service cause it’s really worth it.

Good atmosphere, but a bit pricey
I didn’t like the tuna sashimi dish — it was just two slices of tuna with a handful of chopped green onion
That said, I’d definitely come back for the Europe Jasmine cocktail, it was strong and sweet. Shoutout to the bartender :) !!
Overall, 8/10

Such a great atmosphere of Tel Avivian cafes for beaches & lunches with the beautiful hidden garden in shadow at the courtyard, tasty food, I would recommend nice crab  omelet & cucumber melon green salad, good coffee! The only disadvantage on the menu there are not fresh squeezed juices

drink.name: Coffee
drink.reason: Mentioned positively as strong; Aperol Spritz also praised but coffee fits café context better. Limited specific drink mentions.
drink.quote: coffee strong and the service warm and friendly

food.name: Breakfast platter
food.reason: Praised across multiple 5-star reviews for freshness, variety of Israeli flavors (salads, hummus, eggplants), and refillable bread.
food.quote: nicely served and has a taste of all the Israeli flavours

reviewSample.text:
Cafe Alma was a great experience — the food was fresh, flavorful, and beautifully done. Shaksuka and soft scrambled truffle bruschetta were absolutely delicious! They also have fluffy gluten free bread which was a pleasant surprise for my husband!

What really stood out, though, was the service from Moises. Truly friendly, attentive, and professional, which is rare in Tel Aviv. He made us feel genuinely welcome and elevated the entire meal. We’ll definitely be back!

Perfect spot in the heart of the artsy Neve Tzedek neighborhood. We shared the breakfast platter which comes with your choice of eggs and in true Israeli fashion, various salads (which you can get refills of) and fresh bread (also refillable).
Everything was very fresh, coffee strong and the service warm and friendly (Thanks Moises!)

10/10 reccomend

We ate breakfast here, the service was good and fast. The staff helpful and the food was good, fresh and cooked well. The couples breakfast is nicely served and has a taste of all the Israeli flavours - falafel, hummus, eggplants and more. Recommended little spot in iconic neighborhood.

drink.name: Coffee
drink.reason: Coffee is mentioned positively across nearly every review, described as 'excellent' and a highlight, though no specific drink type is named.
drink.quote: Had excellent coffee and a tasty freshly baked cookie

food.name: Freshly baked cookie
food.reason: Specifically named and praised in a 5-star review; other food mentions are generic (cakes, sandwiches, traditional food) without a clear standout.
food.quote: a tasty freshly baked cookie

reviewSample.text: Cafe Yaffa is such a unique place that beautifully brings culture, books, and coffee together. It’s a space where you meet interesting, diverse people while enjoying a great selection of books. The atmosphere is warm and inspiring, and the coffee, tea, and food are all excellent. A special spot that feels more like a community than just a café. Highly recommended.
I've been to the cafe a few times. Had excellent coffee and a tasty freshly baked cookie this time. Warm and pleasant service. Lovely atmosphere. Also outdoor seating available.

Very nice bookstore and a coffee shop. Great service, very attentive and friendly, nice menu, decent prices. Very clean and cozy, this cafe offers variety of cold and hot drinks, snacks, sandwiches and more. Wi-if is available as well

drink.name: Iced coffee
drink.reason: Specifically called out by a reviewer who doesn't usually like iced coffee but was impressed; otherwise praise is generic 'great coffee' and their home blend beans for Makineta.
drink.quote: not usually an ice coffee drinker but I was pretty impressed

food.reason: Only one vague mention that 'the food is fresh and tasty' with no specific item named.

reviewSample.text: Amazing coffee, fresh squeezed juice ( which is super important for me in my morning routine) and probably the best owner/worker I ever met. Such a funny and welcoming stuff. They make the really enjoyable atmosphere.

A new an upcoming coffee spot that’s worth it’s weight. I’m not usually an ice coffee drinker but I was pretty impressed. Super friendly baristas and overall great experience.. Also, coffee enthusiasts can purchase coffee gear such as grinders, machines and beans!
The coffee was excellent, fair prices, especially for Tel Aviv. High quality glassware and a very friendly team. Definitely give it a try if you're looking to get a coffee in the area

drink.name: Coffee
drink.reason: Multiple high-star reviews praise the coffee as exceptional and well-prepared, though no specific drink type dominates.
drink.quote: coffee was exceptional, honestly among the best we've had in Israel

food.name: Apple croissant
food.reason: Pastries are widely praised across positive reviews, with the apple croissant specifically named and called delicious.
food.quote: delicious pastry (apple croissant)

reviewSample.text:
We discovered this café on Google Maps almost by accident, and it turned out to be a real pleasant surprise.

From the moment we walked in, the place felt calm, tasteful, and thoughtfully designed. Spotlessly clean, beautifully designed menus in Hebrew and English, soft French music in the background, and even small details like the matching plates and table colors added to the atmosphere.

The food was excellent. The quiche was rich yet delicate, and the cake had the kind of balance that comes from a bakery focused on flavor rather than overwhelming sweetness.

And the coffee was exceptional, honestly among the best we’ve had in Israel. Smooth, balanced, and clearly prepared with care.

A beautiful café with excellent food, warm service, and an atmosphere that makes you want to stay a little longer.

There is no place like this in TLV!!!! Chantal brings the best parts of Paris to TLV….all the Parisian chic in a small luxurious cafè. It starts with the warmest welcoming…and goes through the delicious pastries and coffee, all served in the most beautiful dishes and tableware.
DO NOT MISS IT!!!!

The overall bill for a small takeaway americano plus 1 pastr6 was 40 shekels. A tiny pastry was not fresh but most outrageously it cost 24 shekels. 24  shekels for a dry flavourless piece of pastry  the size a bureka. The takeway coffee was watery and terrible.  Please boycott this ridiculous place

drink.name: Coffee
drink.reason: Consistently praised as 'great' or 'excellent' across nearly every review, though no specific drink type is named.
drink.quote: excellent coffee

food.name: Sandwich
food.reason: Sandwiches are the standout item, repeatedly called the highlight—original, delicious, with great bread and vegan options like cauliflower cutlets and brown eggs with eggplant.
food.quote: the highlight of the place is sandwiches

reviewSample.text:
Beautiful new place in the heart of Basel. Fantastic decoration and lovely staff welcoming everyone. The coffee is great but the highlight of the place is sandwiches.. I ate the brown eggs with eggplant and many others tasty things. It’s so delicious ! The bread is amazing.

Highly recommend !

Nice local Cafe in Bazel area. The coffee was great and there are a lot of fresh pastries and sandwiches to choose from. Even though the place was full there are plenty of seating options in the vicinity.

I'm a bit biased since I spent many coffee mornings here. It is a small place that is very busy in the mornings and afternoons. Has great coffee and a lot of vegan/vegetarian options including sophisticated and very good sandwiches.

Also, there is a variety of pastries that are great!

And the option to get the coffee in a glass cup, I hate to get the coffee in a disposable cup.

drink.name: Cappuccino
drink.reason: Only specific drink named in reviews, paired positively with pastries.
drink.quote: a pistachio pastry with a cappuccino. Everything was super tasty!

food.name: Pistachio chocolate croissant
food.reason: Highly praised in a 5-star review; croissants and pistachio pastries are repeatedly celebrated across multiple reviews.
food.quote: The pistachio chocolate croissant is to die for.

reviewSample.text: Very authentic pastry made by French bakers. The papillion has all the crusty, sweetness and salty flavour blended in it. The brioche de Suisse is crust outside but moisture inside (bit too sweet for me though). And quiche de champignon is similarly crust outside and juicy inside. Very friendly barista and good vibe for breakfast at the heart of Tel Aviv-Yafo

It's a beautiful place with great pastries and very friendly people. I like to visit this cafe from time to time. Their unfilled profiteroles are really delicious. reviewSample.text:
The Good
The vegan brioche toast was genuinely well made, with a nut-based cheese that had the right texture and an herb “butter” that added real depth. The presentation was clean and appealing. The coffee was excellent. The “Garden Queen” salad tasted fresh and crisp, even though it came straight from the fridge. The self-service system works smoothly, and the space is friendly for anyone working on a laptop.

The Bad and the Ugly
Prices are on the higher end for this kind of place. Not unreasonable given the location and the quality of the ingredients, but the premium is definitely noticeable.

The Beautiful
The combination of thoughtful presentation, quality ingredients, and a relaxed, work-friendly atmosphere creates a polished and enjoyable experience.

Conclusion
Strong vegan dishes, great coffee, and an efficient setup. The prices sit high, but the overall experience largely justifies the cost.

Excellent Drinks & Vegan Options, But Portions Don't Justify the Price

We visited Anicca on a Wednesday evening, and my experience was a real mix of highs and lows.
The cafe itself is impeccably clean (a 10/10) with a modern, bright feel. It was quiet and perfect for a chat, though the table spacing felt a bit tight.

The service style is very modern: you order everything from a large screen and get a buzzer that rings when your order is ready for pickup.
It was very efficient, and our items were ready in about 10 minutes.

Let's start with the highlights, which were definitely the drinks and dessert.
The Blue Salvia cocktail was amazing-truly refreshing, unique, and delicious.
The gluten-free Citrus Cake was also a standout and a huge plus for anyone looking for high-quality, specialty dessert options.

However, the main issue with our visit was the food, specifically the value.
We ordered the Caesar Wrap, and while the flavor was good, the portion was tiny-it was essentially half a wrap.
This was the core problem: the food is tasty, but the sizes are far too small for the price tag.

The final verdict: We spent 172 ILS for two people and unfortunately left the cafe still feeling hungry.

I'd recommend Anicca if you're vegan or looking for a great, inventive cocktail or a light coffee and cake.
But if you're looking for a full, satisfying meal, you may be disappointed by the value.

A great cafe which is a collaboration between Ilan’s coffee and Tatty bakery and serves wonderful, pastries cakes, and of course, coffee along side with some coffee supplies. We took some coffee to go and some pastries, which were wonderful.

reviewSample.text: The place feels cozy and welcoming, the staff is always friendly, and the coffee is very delicious. You can really feel that they care about quality. I’ve already been here a few times and keep coming back. It’s definitely becoming one of my favorite spots. Highly recommended!

This isn’t just another coffee shop. It’s run by someone who truly lives and breathes coffee. The level of care, knowledge, and consistency is something you don’t come across often. If you’re into coffee (or want to understand what good coffee really tastes like), this place is worth your time.

During a recent visit to Tel-Aviv, I was fortunate to discover a new coffee shop called Loki. My latte was perfect, and the pastries are delicious. Service was 5 stars. I even got to meet one of the owners, Andre. You can really tell the owners care here! I highly recommend 👌

reviewSample.text: Great neighborhood coffee shop. Formerly Cafe 51, now under a new name. They roast their own beans. Lots of pastry options and small sandwiches.

Love this place. Been coming here a lot and the great points are:
Lots of tables, kind workers, great coffee, quiet little corner, electrical sockets almost everywhere.
The weaker points are:
No matter how many fans are turned on outside, it’s hot as hell in the summer. There’s very few inside seating.

The neighborhood spot - you’re guaranteed to see some locals and get used to them here.

Thank you so much!

This place is all about quality coffee. They specialize in fresh single-origin beans and brew them with care. I had a batch brew that was rich and clean. The café itself has a modern but warm vibe, great for working or just chilling out. It’s not loud or trendy—just a solid coffee spot where you know the beans were chosen and roasted with intention.

reviewSample.text: Very nice place in a convenient location, right by a train stop. The owner is friendly and has a great selection of coffee as well as a variety of coffee brewers available for purchase. She has a small sitting area inside as well as a few tables outside where you can enjoy your coffee and maybe strike up a conversation with another visitor if you are so inclined, or just watch the people, cars and trains pass. I visited on Saturday, and it was fairly quiet because the trains weren't running, but I suspect it's can get a little louder when the trains pass.

Stepping into Alia’s boutique café feels like entering a world where coffee tells its own story. The name itself honors the African man who once sowed the first seeds of this beloved drink, grounding the café in heritage and tradition. The coffee’s taste is transporting – bold yet delicate – with an aroma that seems to carry you far beyond the walls of the shop. Alongside the experience, Alia curates a selection of coffee treasures, from authentic macinetas to handpicked beans, inviting every guest not only to enjoy a cup but also to bring a piece of the journey home.
Great coffee place to taste many different types of coffee. The owner is so nice and friendly. Very recommended!

PERFECT !
There are so many places to get sandwiches, cakes, pasteries, coffee, cold drinks, hot chocolate and so on.
Really, Tel aviv is full of them. Each one has a unique sandwich with a twist.

But... only one place has the entire package of: great sandwiches, great cakes, wonderful coffe and above all personal service.
To sum up, Ilan's at the Rothschild Blvd. is a place where you are served to fun food and good coffe.
Go there! I'm sure to return.

Oh, be nice give the boys a tip, they are really doing their best.

reviewSample.text: Me and my partner were drawn in by the incredible smell of garlic and herbs. We were told that the place is not yet open for food, the owner was clearly by himself, stressed and short on time. But as soon as we told him why we came in he hooked us up! Boy oh boy did he deliver ! He cooked us up probably  the best rustic ravioli ive had in my life! Served with great wine and not to mention quick! Dont be fooled by those 1 start reviews, dudes doing his best all by himself and it's totally worth it
